title: Failed to pick output format
author: amirza82
body:
I use cage to start a greeter using greetd. here's the launch command in greetd config:
```
# /etc/greetd/config.toml
...
command = "cage -d -s -m extend -- gtkgreet -s /etc/greetd/gtkgreet.css"
...
```

Cage version is 0.2.1

without any external monitors connected, it initially gives me an empty buffer on eDP-1.
when I kill cage from another tty and greetd service launches it again, output works as expected.

when an external monitor is connected with the -m extend option, eDP-1 still remains empty but HDMI-A-1 does give me the output.
after the aforementioned kill procedure, eDP-1 works again.

# logs:

in all the following logs I first booted my laptop to produce the error. then killed cage and waited for it to restart.

[cage.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29660145/cage.log) (without -m <...>)
[cage-last.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29660143/cage-last.log) (with -m last)
[cage-extend.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29660144/cage-extend.log) (with -m extend)

**These logs are with no external monitor connected.**

The following log is with the external monitor connected and using -m extend command:
[cage-with-HDMI.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29660363/cage-with-HDMI.log)

title: [Bug]: segfault with `-m last`, enabling output during teardown
author: abkein
body:
1. CMD: `dbus-run-session cage -s -d -m last -D -- regreet`
2. I authenticate.
3. ReGreet exits so greetd can start real Wayland session.
4. Cage begins teardown.
5. During teardown, Cage crashes in the `-m last` multi-output path.

The key stack is:

```text
wl_list_insert
output_layout_add
handle_output_destroy
wlr_output_finish
drm_connector_destroy_output
backend_destroy
wl_display_destroy
main
```

In Cage 0.3.0, [output_destroy](https://github.com/cage-kiosk/cage/blob/master/output.c) does this:

```c
} else if (server->output_mode == CAGE_MULTI_OUTPUT_MODE_LAST && !wl_list_empty(&server->outputs)) {
    struct cg_output *prev = wl_container_of(server->outputs.next, prev, link);
    output_enable(prev);
    view_position_all(server);
}
```

So when one output is destroyed, Cage tries to re-enable another output. That makes sense for hotplug while running, but during compositor/backend destruction it is unsafe.

title: seat: unable to configure keyboard

body:
This might not be a cage specific problem per se, but I haven't came across it anywhere else. The `/usr/local/share` hierarchy is not populated on my system. This yields an error in https://github.com/cage-kiosk/cage/blob/79e1e0dfaad77f1ca096349a0f8b2666fbe3ee03/seat.c#L402 (HEAD at the time of writing). When debugging, I noticed context (constructed a few lines above) gets a new item in `fail_includes` of  `"/home/username/.config/xkb"`. That indeed seems like an invalid include. Expected behavior would be to get `/usr/share/X11/xkb` in `includes` instead.

```
(gdb) print *context
$12 = {refcnt = 1, log_fn = 0xfffff7dd4b4c <default_log_fn>, log_level = XKB_LOG_LEVEL_ERROR, log_verbosity = 0, user_data = 0x0, names_dflt = {

$13 = 0xb6ceb0 "/home/username/.config/xkb"
```

title: Add support for `xdg-decoration` version 2
author: danielfikko
body: The version 2 allows the client to enable or disable window decorations at runtime. See for more information: https://gitlab.freedesktop.org/wayland/wayland-protocols/-/work_items/9 https://gitlab.freedesktop.org/wayland/wayland-protocols/-/commit/fedde2456ed1572ad4d46fff119220cb845e79d6 In `winex11`, there is an option to disable or enable window decorations: One of the reasons `winewayland` does not support server-side decorations is the lack of compositors implementing version 2 of the `xdg-decoration` protocol, which would facilitate the implementation of this Wine option. If, even in version 1, you already allow clients to change window decorations at runtime, all you need to do is simply bump the version from 1 to 2, just like KDE Plasma and Jay: https://github.com/mahkoh/jay/pull/759 https://invent.kde.org/plasma/kwin/-/merge_requests/8739

title: [Bug] Cursor hides in libmpv video and does not reappear
author: ferguscollins
body:
## Behaviour

### Expected behaviour

Cursor never gets hidden while a pointing device is connected

### Actual behaviour

Using `stremio-linux-shell` which uses `libmpv` for video playback, the cursor hides when playback begins and the cursor is static. The cursor then never reappears if the player is paused or closed and the mouse is moved.

As far as I can tell, the cursor not reappearing is expected behaviour due to `update_capabilities()` only changing cursor state based on pointing device availability (I could be wrong here).

The cursor disappearing in the first place is not expected behaviour however.

### Preferred behaviour

Let the cursor get hidden but redraw it when the pointing device provides an input.

## Solution

An actual fix would be to figure out why the cursor is being hidden and not letting that happen, but the feature is actually useful so I would prefer the following solution:

- Check if the cursor needs to be redrawn on pointing device input

If this causes a lot of overhead then perhaps an off-by-default environment variable could be used.

title: Can you use Cage as a wayland sandbox?

body:
Some people recommend Cage as part of a Wayland sandboxing toolkit:

To sandbox a Wayland program, you might use [Bubblewrap](https://wiki.archlinux.org/title/Bubblewrap). But that alone is not enough because direct access to the Wayland socket has security problems. To fix that you might use Cage to wrap the socket.

What do you think of this usage? Are there any problems with tit? Is it one of Cage's goals to support this? How does it compare to `wp_security_context_v1`?

You might not be able to answer all of these questions. That's fine. I'm just looking for some context from the maintainer about this.

title: XCURSOR_THEME support
author: tjallingt
body:
The man page describes that cage supports `XCURSOR_*` environment variables:

https://github.com/cage-kiosk/cage/blob/master/cage.1.scd

> # ENVIRONMENT
> 
> _DISPLAY_
> 	If compiled with Xwayland support, this will be set to the name of the
> 	X display used for Xwayland. Otherwise, probe the X11 backend.
> 
> _WAYLAND_DISPLAY_
> 	Specifies the name of the Wayland display that Cage is running on.
> 
> _XCURSOR_PATH_
> 	Directory where cursors are located.
> 
> _XCURSOR_SIZE_
> 	Specifies the configured cursor size.
> 
> _XCURSOR_THEME_
> 	Specifies the configured cursor theme.
> 
> _XKB_DEFAULT_RULES_, _XKB_DEFAULT_MODEL_, _XKB_DEFAULT_LAYOUT_,
> _XKB_DEFAULT_VARIANT_, _XKB_DEFAULT_OPTIONS_
> 	Configures the xkb keyboard settings. See *xkeyboard-config*(7).


However in issues https://github.com/cage-kiosk/cage/issues/299 and https://github.com/cage-kiosk/cage/issues/353 users are saying that these environment variables are not actually used.

I'm attempting to use the `xcursor-transparent` theme build by this yocto recipe https://github.com/openembedded/openembedded-core/blob/master/meta/recipes-graphics/xcursor-transparent-theme/xcursor-transparent-theme_git.bb and setting `XCURSOR_THEME=xcursor-transparent` does not appear to have an effect.

It would be nice if this could be fixed, or otherwise the manpage should probably be updated.

Cage version 0.1.5

title: [question] screen capture solutions
author: travisghansen
body:
Curious if anyone knows of a way to capture the screen content systemically and without any kind of user intervention?

I have an IoT device which launches cage and depending on config it will launch chrome (controlled via remote debug), kodi, mpv, or others. The management of the device is done entirely remotely via mobile app and a custom control-plane daemon that run on the device. The device takes a screenshot of the desktop (iirc using grim) once every 30s to send to a server-side component for all devices. So I store a low-res screenshot in the backend which is displayed in the app to show ‘current’ content.

What I would like to do is wire up the wayland session to a webrtc (sfu) peer and then let the app connect to the sfu to get a realtime stream of the desktop data. Feasible? I could probably create snapshots with a high frequency and start pumping those over a webrtc track but that feels icky.
